description: The Chinese Communist Party under Mao Zedong proclaimed the People's Republic of China on October 1, 1949, after defeating the Nationalist government. This transformed the world's most populous country and had enormous consequences for global geopolitics throughout the Cold War.

name: Korean War
description: North Korea (backed by China and USSR) invaded South Korea (backed by US-led UN coalition). Ended in armistice — technically still not over. Approximately 5 million casualties. Established the current division of the Korean Peninsula at the 38th parallel.

name: Vietnam War
description: Communist North Vietnam and Viet Cong vs South Vietnam and US forces. Over 58,000 American and 3 million Vietnamese deaths. US withdrawal after public opposition at home. North Vietnam unified the country under communist rule in 1975.

name: Suez Crisis
description: Egypt's President Nasser nationalized the Suez Canal. Britain, France, and Israel invaded. US and Soviet pressure forced a withdrawal. A landmark moment for Arab nationalism and the decline of European colonial power.

name: Six-Day War
description: Israel launched a pre-emptive strike against Egypt, Jordan, and Syria, capturing the Sinai, Gaza, West Bank, and Golan Heights in six days. Jerusalem's Old City came under Israeli control. Reshaped the Middle East conflict for decades.

name: Moon Landing — Apollo 11
description: Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin became the first humans to walk on the Moon on July 20, 1969. Transmitted live to 600 million viewers worldwide. Armstrong's words — 'One small step for man, one giant leap for mankind' — became iconic. A defining achievement of the 20th century.

name: Invention of the Internet (ARPANET)
description: ARPANET, the precursor to the Internet, sent its first message on October 29, 1969. The development of TCP/IP protocols (1983) and the World Wide Web (1991) transformed it into the global internet — arguably the most transformative communications technology in history.

name: Yom Kippur War
description: Egypt and Syria launched a surprise attack on Israeli positions on Yom Kippur (October 1973). Initial Arab successes were reversed by Israeli counter-offensives. Led to the Camp David Accords (1978) and eventually Egypt-Israel peace (1979).

name: Soviet-Afghan War
description: Soviet Union invaded Afghanistan to support the communist government. Afghan Mujahideen, backed by the US, Pakistan, and Saudi Arabia, waged a decade-long guerrilla war. The Soviets withdrew in 1989. The war contributed to the USSR's collapse and gave rise to the Taliban.

name: Iran-Iraq War
description: Iraq under Saddam Hussein invaded Iran after the Islamic Revolution. Eight years of devastating trench warfare killed approximately one million people. Ended in a ceasefire with no territorial changes — a catastrophic conflict with no clear victor.

name: Falklands War
description: Brief war between Argentina and the United Kingdom over the Falkland Islands (Malvinas). Argentina invaded in April 1982; Britain retook the islands after a 74-day conflict. A British victory that strengthened Thatcher's government but led to Argentina's military junta collapse.

name: Fall of the Berlin Wall
description: The Berlin Wall, symbol of the Cold War and Iron Curtain, fell on November 9, 1989 as East Germany opened its borders. Crowds demolished the wall. Germany was reunified in 1990. Led directly to the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991.

name: Gulf War
description: Iraq invaded Kuwait in August 1990. A US-led coalition of 35 nations launched Operation Desert Storm, liberating Kuwait in 100 hours of ground combat. Iraq's military was devastated. Saddam remained in power but under severe UN sanctions.

name: Apartheid Ends in South Africa
description: South Africa's apartheid system of racial segregation ended with Nelson Mandela's release from prison (1990) and the first democratic elections (April 1994). Mandela became president. The peaceful transition was seen as a global model for reconciliation and justice.

name: Dissolution of the Soviet Union
description: On December 25, 1991, Mikhail Gorbachev resigned and the USSR dissolved into 15 independent republics. Ended the Cold War. Russia emerged as the principal successor state. The US remained the world's sole superpower. Reshaped global geopolitics fundamentally.

name: Formation of the World Wide Web
description: Tim Berners-Lee launched the World Wide Web in 1991, making the Internet accessible to the public. The information revolution that followed transformed communications, commerce, education, politics, and society more profoundly than any technology since the printing press.

name: Bosnian War
description: Following Yugoslavia's breakup, Bosnian Serb forces conducted ethnic cleansing of Bosnian Muslims (Bosniaks) and Croats. The Srebrenica massacre (1995) killed over 8,000 Bosniak men. NATO intervention and the Dayton Agreement ended the conflict.

name: European Union Founded (Maastricht Treaty)
description: The Maastricht Treaty (1992) came into force on November 1, 1993, formally establishing the European Union. The EU grew to 27 member states, created a single market and eventually a common currency (the Euro, 1999), representing the most ambitious political and economic integration in history.

name: Rwandan Genocide
description: In 100 days, approximately 800,000 Tutsi and moderate Hutu Rwandans were massacred by Hutu extremists. One of the fastest mass killings in history. International community failed to intervene. Led to major reforms in international humanitarian law.

name: Kosovo War
description: Conflict between Yugoslavia's government forces and the Kosovo Liberation Army (KLA), ending with NATO bombing of Yugoslavia and international administration of Kosovo. Led to Kosovo's eventual independence in 2008.

name: War in Afghanistan (Post-9/11)
description: US-led coalition invaded Afghanistan to topple the Taliban regime after the September 11 attacks. The Taliban were removed but waged an insurgency for 20 years. US and NATO forces withdrew in 2021; the Taliban retook Kabul within days.

name: September 11 Attacks and Global War on Terror
description: Al-Qaeda attacked the US on September 11, 2001, killing nearly 3,000 people in New York, Washington DC, and Pennsylvania. Launched the US-led Global War on Terror, reshaping global security, privacy laws, and international relations.

name: September 11, 2001 Attacks
description: Al-Qaeda hijacked four planes in the US on September 11, 2001. Two struck the World Trade Center in New York, one hit the Pentagon, and one crashed in Pennsylvania. 2,977 killed. The defining event of the early 21st century, triggering the War on Terror and reshaping global security.

name: Iraq War
description: US-led coalition invaded Iraq, alleging weapons of mass destruction. Saddam Hussein was overthrown and later executed (2006). Subsequent years saw devastating sectarian civil war and the rise of al-Qaeda in Iraq — precursor to ISIS.

name: Smartphone and Digital Revolution
description: Apple launched the iPhone in 2007, ushering in the smartphone era and transforming human communication, commerce, and culture. Combined with social media platforms (Facebook 2004, Twitter 2006), the digital revolution reshaped how billions of people interact, work, and access information.

name: Arab Spring
description: Wave of pro-democracy protests across the Arab world beginning in Tunisia (December 2010). Toppled governments in Tunisia, Egypt, Libya, and Yemen. Led to civil wars in Syria and Libya. Followed by military coups and counter-revolutions in several countries.

name: Syrian Civil War
description: Uprising against President Bashar al-Assad escalated into a devastating multi-sided civil war involving Syrian government, rebel factions, ISIS, Kurdish forces, and foreign powers. Over 500,000 killed and 13 million displaced in one of the worst humanitarian crises of the 21st century.

name: Rise and Fall of ISIS (Islamic State)
description: ISIS/ISIL declared a caliphate across Iraq and Syria in 2014, briefly controlling territory the size of Great Britain. Known for extreme brutality. Defeated militarily by 2019 through a coalition including the US, Iraq, Syria, and Kurdish forces, though the insurgency continued.

name: COVID-19 Global Pandemic
description: COVID-19, caused by the SARS-CoV-2 coronavirus, was first identified in Wuhan, China in late 2019. By 2020 it became a global pandemic, killing approximately 7 million people officially (possibly 15-20 million excess deaths). Caused the worst global economic disruption since the Great Depression.

name: Russia-Ukraine War
description: Russia launched a full-scale invasion of Ukraine on February 24, 2022, after annexing Crimea in 2014. The largest war in Europe since World War II. Ukraine mounted fierce resistance with Western support. Tens of thousands killed and millions displaced.

name: Russia Invades Ukraine (2022)
description: Russia launched a full-scale invasion of Ukraine on February 24, 2022, the largest military attack in Europe since World War II. Ukraine, with Western military and financial support, mounted fierce resistance. Ongoing conflict with massive casualties, displacement, and global economic consequences.

name: Gaza War (2023)
description: Following a Hamas attack on southern Israel on October 7, 2023, killing approximately 1,200 Israelis, Israel launched a massive military campaign in Gaza. One of the deadliest conflicts in the Palestinian-Israeli history, causing massive civilian casualties and a severe humanitarian crisis.
